message:
deps(fnp-conformance): bump sha2 from 0.10.9 to 0.11.0

crates/fnp-conformance/Cargo.toml: sha2 0.10.9 -> 0.11.0 (latest stable).

sha2 0.11 highlights (from RustCrypto/hashes CHANGELOG):
- Updates underlying `digest` crate from 0.10 to 0.11.
- Converts hash types (Sha256, Sha512, ...) from type aliases to newtype
  structs; this only affects pattern matching / deep type inference, not
  our call style.
- `compress256`/`compress512` relocated from root to `block_api` module.
- Feature flags `asm`, `asm-aarch64`, `loongarch64_asm`, `compress`,
  `soft`, `force-soft-compact`, and `std` removed; new `alloc` feature
  replaces `std` for allocator-dependent code.
- MSRV bumped to 1.85 (edition 2024); we're already on edition 2024 so
  this is fine.

fnp-conformance only uses `sha2::Digest::digest(bytes)`,
`Sha256::new()`, `hasher.update(...)`, `hasher.finalize()`. These are
preserved through the `Digest` trait in digest 0.11, so no call-site
changes are required.

Transitive removals pulled by `cargo update -p sha2@0.10.9
--precise 0.11.0`:
  - block-buffer 0.10.4
  - cpufeatures 0.2.17
  - crypto-common 0.1.7
  - digest 0.10.7
  - generic-array 0.14.7
  - sha2 0.10.9
(replaced by digest 0.11 + its new hybrid-array-based internals.)

Verified:
  rch exec -- cargo check -p fnp-conformance --all-targets  (clean)
  rch exec -- cargo test  -p fnp-conformance --lib raptorq  (4/4 pass;
    the raptorq_artifacts module is the sha2 consumer.)

A broader `cargo test -p fnp-conformance --lib` does show 3 pre-existing
failures in `test_contract_suite_is_green` / `core_suites_are_green`
that all panic with
  "linalg_differential_cases invalid fixture id
   linalg_cholesky_solve_identity_L_returns_b"
These are fixture-registry failures owned by another agent: the ID is
only present in fixtures/linalg_differential_cases.json and not
registered in the linalg fixture ID enum. They are unrelated to sha2
and reproduce independently of this bump. Documented in UPGRADE_LOG.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -29,6 +29,12 @@
 - **Cargo.lock:** already at 1.1.9 (transitive refresh from a prior session pulled it forward; manifest caught up here).
 - **Verified:** `cargo check -p fnp-io --all-targets` pass. `cargo test -p fnp-io` 222/222 pass.
 
+#### sha2: 0.10.9 -> 0.11.0 (fnp-conformance)
+
+- **Research:** sha2 0.11 updates to `digest` 0.11 and converts hash types (`Sha256`, `Sha512`, ...) from type aliases to newtype structs. Module reorg: `compress256`/`compress512` moved to `block_api`. Features `asm`/`asm-aarch64`/`loongarch64_asm`/`compress`/`soft`/`force-soft-compact`/`std` removed; new `alloc` feature. MSRV bumped to 1.85 (we're on edition 2024/nightly — fine).
+- **fnp-conformance usage audit:** only `use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};` + `Sha256::digest(bytes)` / `Sha256::new()` / `hasher.update(...)` / `hasher.finalize()`. These APIs are preserved in 0.11 via the `Digest` trait; the newtype conversion does not affect this call style. Transitive removals: `block-buffer`, `cpufeatures`, `crypto-common`, `digest 0.10.x`, `generic-array 0.14.x` (all replaced by digest 0.11 / hybrid-array internals).
+- **Verified:** `cargo check -p fnp-conformance --all-targets` passes cleanly. Targeted `cargo test -p fnp-conformance --lib raptorq` (4 tests that exercise the sha2 code paths via `raptorq_artifacts::sha256_hex`) passes 4/4. A broader `cargo test -p fnp-conformance --lib` shows 3 pre-existing failures in `test_contracts::*test_contract_suite_is_green` and `tests::core_suites_are_green` — all complaining about `linalg_differential_cases invalid fixture id linalg_cholesky_solve_identity_L_returns_b`, which is a fixture-registry / data-file issue entirely unrelated to sha2. Confirmed by grep: the failing ID is only defined in `fixtures/linalg_differential_cases.json` and is not registered in the linalg fixture ID enum — pre-existing breakage owned by another agent / the linalg team.
+
 ---
 
 ## 2026-02-20 Session (legacy)

-sha2 = "0.10.9"
+sha2 = "0.11.0"
